pr.title: Rename previously colliding .o files and add build/ to gitignore
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Renamed three assembly source/object entries that previously used trailing underscores to avoid filename collisions, updating obj_files.mk from string_.o/debug_.o/psdisptev_.o to string.o/debug.o/psdisptev.o. Also added build/ to .gitignore so generated build directories are not tracked, and normalized the missing final newline in .gitignore.
what_changed: Added build/ under the Build artifacts section of .gitignore.
Changed TEXT_O_FILES entries in obj_files.mk from $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/string_.o to $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/string.o, from debug_.o to debug.o, and from psdisptev_.o to psdisptev.o.
Renamed asm/string_.s to asm/string.s with 100% similarity.
Renamed asm/debug_.s to asm/debug.s with 100% similarity.
Renamed asm/psdisptev_.s to asm/psdisptev.s with 100% similarity.
smart_moves: Kept the assembly file renames content-preserving, with 100% similarity, minimizing behavioral risk while fixing naming/build issues.
Updated obj_files.mk in the same PR as the physical asm file renames, keeping source filenames and build object paths synchronized.
Added build/ to .gitignore alongside existing generated artifacts (*.o, *.elf, *.map), preventing local build output from polluting future diffs.
decomp_lessons: When placeholder assembly files are renamed, update both the filesystem path and TEXT_O_FILES so the build graph remains coherent.
Prefer canonical object/source names once collisions are resolved instead of keeping temporary suffixes such as trailing underscores.
Repository hygiene changes like ignoring build/ are valuable in decomp projects because generated artifacts can be large and noisy.
naming_conventions: Temporary collision-avoidance suffix pattern observed: string_.s/debug_.s/psdisptev_.s and matching .o entries.
Canonicalized names remove the trailing underscore: string.s, debug.s, psdisptev.s.
Build object entries follow $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/<basename>.o for assembly files listed in TEXT_O_FILES.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR did not change assembly contents; the matching tactic was a pure rename of existing asm stubs with 100% similarity.
Keeping object basename changes aligned with source basename changes avoids accidental build mismatches after asm file renames.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that depend on canonical asm names string.s, debug.s, or psdisptev.s.
Search for remaining asm files or obj_files.mk entries with trailing underscore suffixes that may represent old collision workarounds.
Check whether build/ remains ignored consistently after later build-system restructures.
